title: Thread management
creator: chris.a1
content:
I use Thunderbird and have done for years. Nowadays new messages seem to be placed in the inbox within previous email threads within that thread. This often means that they are not flagged as new emails. They get buried in old threads and never brought to notice, consequently are never seen. This means I miss a lot of emails.

Is there any selectable option that brings all messages into the inbox individually and visibly, so that they are not missed, and always seen. That is, stops emails being placed within an existing thread, no matter what its content is. I am very happy to reorganise my incoming mails manually.

title: I can't connect to email from Bolivia
creator: stefrose
content:
Here's what I see: This is a very specific server. We tried connecting to your servers from various IP addresses, but all connection attempts were rejected with error messages similar to the one you sent me. I get the impression that you need to contact them to ask them to accept connections from the IP addresses to which emails are to be sent. I suggest contacting your point of contact at this domain through other means, whether by phone, WhatsApp, or another email address they receive—perhaps one from Germany—and asking your provider to adjust the settings so that emails sent from the IP address 148.251.119.178 are accepted. You can point out that our server is legitimate, does not send spam, is not on any blacklists, and uses all protocols for validating email delivery, including SPF, DKIM, and DMARC.

This email address cannot send me emails because they keep getting bounced back to the sender. All other email addresses work, just this one doesn't.

title: Two changes to spam filter
creator: jsg11
content:
A couple of suggestions.

1) After opening an email marked as spam (by Thunderbird's spam filter), there is no option to confirm that this is, indeed spam. The email can be marked as "Not spam," but not confirmed to be spam. Unless I'm missing something, I have to go back to the inbox, mark it as spam manually, and then it is automatically sent to the spam box.

2) Assuming I understand the spam filtering correctly, once Thunderbird marks emails as spam, they remain in the inbox for at least 1 day (or longer if desired). I would like to have those emails sent IMMEDIATELY to the spam box, where I can still examine them to make sure they're spam before deleting them.

That way, the inbox will have only emails that either are not spam at all and those that haven't been marked as such.

As the system now works -- unless I'm misusing it somehow -- once the filter marks emails as spam, I still have to wade through them to sort out the good ones. In my view, that pretty much defeats the purpose of having the filter in the first place. I still have to "mark" them as spam again to get them sent to the spam box.

Perhaps I should note that this inbox gets dozens of spam emails every day and that I check it multiple times per day.
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:149.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/149.0;tb_version:149.0.1 (64-bit);os:Windows 11;solver_id:2738226

creator: g8daniele
content: Da 6 mesi circa nei promemoria che contengono le date di compleanni, (inseriti nei Contatti telefonici), non si riesce a posticiparli o eliminarli, compare la dicitura: Si è verificato un errore durante la scrittura del calendario [RIMOSSO]@gmail.com. Server Replied with 400 . L'unico sistema per risolvere momentaneamente il problema è quello di eliminare del tutto il contatto dall'Agenda Telefonica e poi reinserirlo.
